# ForgeSpec MCP

> The coordination backbone for multi-agent AI development. MCP Server for Spec-Driven Development (SDD) — provides typed contract validation, SQLite-backed task board management, and advisory file locking to coordinate work across multiple AI agents. Built on the Model Context Protocol SDK with better-sqlite3 and Zod schema validation.

## Why ForgeSpec?

Building software with multiple AI agents introduces coordination challenges that don't exist in single-agent workflows:

| Problem | Without ForgeSpec | With ForgeSpec |
|---------|-------------------|----------------|
| Conflicting edits | Two agents modify the same file, causing merge conflicts | File reservation with TTL prevents conflicts |
| No shared context | Each agent works in isolation | Contract validation creates shared audit trail |
| Unstructured work | Agents jump to code without specs | 9-phase pipeline enforces structured flow |
| Lost progress | No way to resume after failure | SQLite task board persists state |
| No quality gates | Code ships without validation | Confidence thresholds block premature transitions |

**Key advantages:**
- Zero infrastructure — Embedded SQLite, no external services required
- Universal compatibility — Any MCP client (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, OpenClaw)
- Instant setup — One command: `npx -y forgespec-mcp`
- Battle-tested pipeline — 9 phases with confidence thresholds
- Full audit trail — Every contract, task, and reservation is logged with timestamps
- Cross-platform — Tested on Ubuntu, Windows, macOS with Node 18, 20, 22
- Cortex-ready — Native integration with [Cortex](https://github.com/lleontor705/cortex) for persistent memory and knowledge graph

## Recommended: Pair with Cortex

ForgeSpec manages the **workflow** (contracts, tasks, file locks). [Cortex](https://github.com/lleontor705/cortex) manages the **memory** (observations, knowledge graph, session continuity). Together they form a complete multi-agent coordination stack:

- **ForgeSpec** validates SDD contracts, manages task dependencies, prevents file conflicts
- **Cortex** stores artifacts as observations, connects them via knowledge graph, enables session recovery
- Artifacts saved with `type: "cortex"` are persisted to Cortex via `mem_save` and linked with `mem_relate`
- ForgeSpec works standalone without Cortex — artifacts can also use `type: "openspec"` (filesystem) or `type: "inline"` (returned in response)

## Overview

forgespec-mcp is an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server designed for multi-agent AI development workflows. It enforces a structured 9-phase development pipeline through typed contracts, manages tasks with intelligent dependency resolution, and provides advisory file locking for agent coordination.

**Key characteristics:**
- TypeScript, compiled to ES2022 with Node16 module resolution
- SQLite database with WAL mode for concurrent access
- Zod runtime schema validation for all inputs
- Runs as a stdio MCP transport (launched by client applications)
- Node.js >= 18 required

## Installation and Usage

### Using npx (no installation required)

```bash
npx -y forgespec-mcp
```

### Install globally

```bash
npm install -g forgespec-mcp
```

### Claude Code

```bash
claude mcp add forgespec --transport stdio -- npx -y forgespec-mcp
```

### Codex CLI (`~/.codex/config.toml`)

```toml
[mcp_servers.forgespec]
command = "npx"
args = ["-y", "forgespec-mcp"]
```

### Gemini CLI (`settings.json`)

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "forgespec": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "forgespec-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### OpenClaw (`openclaw.json`)

```json5
mcp: {
  servers: {
    forgespec: { command: "npx", args: ["-y", "forgespec-mcp"] }
  }
}
```

## SDD Pipeline

The 9-phase Spec-Driven Development lifecycle:

```
init → explore → propose → spec → design → tasks → apply → verify → archive
```

Each phase has confidence thresholds and allowed transitions enforced by the contract validator.

| Phase | Confidence Threshold | Can Transition To | Purpose |
|-------|---------------------|-------------------|---------|
| init | 0.5 | explore, propose | Bootstrap project context and conventions |
| explore | 0.5 | propose, spec | Investigate codebase, diagnose issues |
| propose | 0.7 | spec, design, init | Draft change proposal with scope and risks |
| spec | 0.8 | design, tasks | Write detailed specifications with Given/When/Then |
| design | 0.7 | tasks, spec | Define architecture, data flows, file changes |
| tasks | 0.8 | apply | Decompose into dependency-ordered implementation tasks |
| apply | 0.6 | verify, tasks | Execute implementation (partial completion allowed) |
| verify | 0.9 | archive, apply | Validate implementation against specs |
| archive | 0.9 | — | Merge specs, generate retrospective |

### SDD Contract Schema

Every SDD contract follows this structure:

```typescript
{
  schema_version: string;           // defaults to "1.0"
  phase: SddPhase;                 // init|explore|propose|spec|design|tasks|apply|verify|archive
  change_name: string;              // 1-256 chars, identifies the change
  project: string;                  // 1-256 chars, project identifier
  status: "success" | "partial" | "failed" | "blocked";
  confidence: number;               // 0.0-1.0 range
  executive_summary: string;        // 10-65536 chars
  artifacts_saved: Artifact[];      // max 50 items
  next_recommended: SddPhase[];     // max 9 phases
  risks: Risk[];                    // max 50 items
  data: Record<string, unknown>;   // arbitrary metadata
}
```

**Risk object:**
```typescript
{ description: string; level: "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical"; }
```

**Artifact object:**
```typescript
{ topic_key: string; type: "cortex" | "openspec" | "inline"; path?: string; }
```

## Tools Reference (19 total)

### SDD Contract Tools (6 tools)

#### `sdd_validate`
Validate an SDD contract against the phase schema. Returns validation result with confidence check and allowed transitions.

**Parameters:**
- `contract` (string, required): JSON string of the SDD contract (max 128KB)

**Returns:** `{ valid, phase, confidence, threshold, meets_confidence, allowed_next_phases, warnings }` or `{ valid: false, errors }`.

**Example:**
```jsonc
// Input
{ "contract": "{\"phase\":\"propose\",\"change_name\":\"add-auth\",\"project\":\"my-app\",\"status\":\"success\",\"confidence\":0.85,\"executive_summary\":\"Add JWT auth service with login/logout endpoints.\"}" }

// Output
{ "valid": true, "phase": "propose", "confidence": 0.85, "threshold": 0.7, "meets_confidence": true, "allowed_next_phases": ["spec", "design", "init"], "warnings": [] }
```

#### `sdd_save`
Validate and persist an SDD contract to the database. The contract must pass validation before it is saved.

**Parameters:**
- `contract` (string, required): JSON string of the SDD contract (max 128KB)

**Returns:** `{ saved: true, id, phase, project }` or `{ saved: false, error }`.

**Example:**
```jsonc
// Input
{ "contract": "{\"phase\":\"propose\",\"change_name\":\"add-auth\",\"project\":\"my-app\",\"status\":\"success\",\"confidence\":0.85,\"executive_summary\":\"Add JWT auth service.\"}" }

// Output
{ "saved": true, "id": "sdd_a1b2c3d4-...", "phase": "propose", "project": "my-app" }
```

#### `sdd_get`
Retrieve a single SDD contract by its ID.

**Parameters:**
- `contract_id` (string, required): Contract ID

**Returns:** Full contract data or `{ error: "Contract {id} not found" }`.

#### `sdd_list`
List all contracts with optional filters.

**Parameters:**
- `project` (string, optional): Filter by project name (alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores)
- `phase` (string, optional): Filter by SDD phase
- `limit` (number, optional): Max entries (1-100, default 20)

**Returns:** `{ contracts: [...], count }`.

#### `sdd_history`
Get the phase transition history for a project, ordered chronologically.

**Parameters:**
- `project` (string, required): Project name (alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores)
- `limit` (number, optional): Max entries (1-100, default 20)

**Returns:** `{ project, history: [{ id, phase, change_name, status, confidence, executive_summary, created_at }] }`.

**Example:**
```jsonc
// Input
{ "project": "my-app", "limit": 3 }

// Output
{ "project": "my-app", "history": [
  { "id": "sdd_...", "phase": "verify", "change_name": "add-auth", "status": "success", "confidence": 0.92, "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:45:00Z" },
  { "id": "sdd_...", "phase": "apply", "change_name": "add-auth", "status": "success", "confidence": 0.78, "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z" },
  { "id": "sdd_...", "phase": "spec", "change_name": "add-auth", "status": "success", "confidence": 0.85, "created_at": "2025-01-15T09:00:00Z" }
]}
```

#### `sdd_phases`
Get all phase definitions with their allowed transitions and confidence thresholds. No parameters required.

**Returns:** `{ phases: [{ phase, confidence_threshold, can_transition_to }] }`.

### Task Board Tools (10 tools)

#### `tb_create_board`
Create a new task board for a project.

**Parameters:**
- `project` (string, required): Project identifier (alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores)
- `name` (string, required): Board name (max 256 chars)

**Returns:** `{ created: true, board_id, project, name }`.

**Example:**
```jsonc
// Input
{ "project": "my-app", "name": "add-auth-service" }

// Output
{ "created": true, "board_id": "board_x7k9m2...", "project": "my-app", "name": "add-auth-service" }
```

#### `tb_add_task`
Add a task to a board with priority, spec reference, acceptance criteria, and dependencies.

**Parameters:**
- `board_id` (string, required): Board ID
- `title` (string, required): Task title (3-512 chars)
- `description` (string, optional): Task description (max 65536 chars)
- `priority` (string, optional): p0 (critical), p1 (high), p2 (medium, default), p3 (low)
- `spec_ref` (string, optional): Reference to a spec artifact (max 512 chars)
- `acceptance_criteria` (string, optional): Criteria for task completion (max 65536 chars)
- `dependencies` (string[], optional): Array of task IDs this task depends on

**Returns:** `{ created: true, task_id, board_id, title, priority }`.

**Example:**
```jsonc
// Input
{ "board_id": "board_x7k9m2...", "title": "Create JWT utility module", "priority": "p0", "spec_ref": "sdd/add-auth/spec", "acceptance_criteria": "All token operations pass unit tests", "dependencies": [] }

// Output
{ "created": true, "task_id": "task_abc123...", "board_id": "board_x7k9m2...", "title": "Create JWT utility module", "priority": "p0" }
```

#### `tb_status`
Get board status with tasks grouped by their current status.

**Parameters:**
- `board_id` (string, required): Board ID

**Returns:** `{ board, tasks: { backlog, ready, in_progress, in_review, done, blocked }, summary: { total, by_status } }`.

#### `tb_claim`
Claim a task for an agent. Verifies all dependencies are met before allowing claim. Only tasks in `ready` or `backlog` status can be claimed.

**Parameters:**
- `task_id` (string, required): Task ID
- `agent` (string, required): Agent identifier (alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores)

**Returns:** `{ claimed: true, task_id, agent, status: "in_progress" }` or error with blockers.

**Example:**
```jsonc
// Input
{ "task_id": "task_abc123...", "agent": "implement-agent-1" }

// Output (success)
{ "claimed": true, "task_id": "task_abc123...", "agent": "implement-agent-1", "status": "in_progress" }

// Output (blocked)
{ "error": "Blocked by unfinished dependencies", "blockers": [{ "id": "task_xyz...", "status": "in_progress" }] }
```

#### `tb_update`
Update task status. When a task is marked `done`, all dependent tasks with no remaining blockers are automatically moved to `ready`. Moving to `done` requires the task to be in `in_progress` or `in_review`.

**Parameters:**
- `task_id` (string, required): Task ID
- `status` (string, required): New status (backlog, ready, in_progress, in_review, done, blocked)
- `notes` (string, optional): Notes about the update (max 65536 chars)

**Returns:** `{ updated: true, task_id, status, unblocked_tasks, notes }`.

**Example:**
```jsonc
// Input
{ "task_id": "task_abc123...", "status": "done", "notes": "JWT module complete with RS256" }

// Output
{ "updated": true, "task_id": "task_abc123...", "status": "done", "unblocked_tasks": ["task_def456..."], "notes": "JWT module complete with RS256" }
```

#### `tb_unblocked`
List tasks that are ready to work on — no unresolved dependencies.

**Parameters:**
- `board_id` (string, required): Board ID

**Returns:** `{ board_id, unblocked_count, tasks: [...] }`.

#### `tb_get`
Get full task details by ID.

**Parameters:**
- `task_id` (string, required): Task ID

**Returns:** `{ task: { id, board_id, title, description, status, priority, assignee, spec_ref, acceptance_criteria, dependencies, notes, created_at, updated_at, claimed_at, completed_at } }`.

#### `tb_delete_task`
Delete a task. Only tasks in `backlog` or `done` status can be deleted. Automatically removes this task from other tasks' dependency lists.

**Parameters:**
- `task_id` (string, required): Task ID

**Returns:** `{ deleted: true, task_id }`.

#### `tb_add_notes`
Append timestamped notes to a task without changing its status.

**Parameters:**
- `task_id` (string, required): Task ID
- `notes` (string, required): Note text (max 65536 chars)

**Returns:** `{ added: true, task_id, notes_count }`.

#### `tb_list`
List all boards, optionally filtered by project.

**Parameters:**
- `project` (string, optional): Filter by project name

**Returns:** `{ boards: [...] }`.

### File Reservation Tools (3 tools)

#### `file_reserve`
Reserve files or glob patterns to prevent conflicts between agents. Reservations expire after the specified TTL. Checks for conflicts before reserving.

**Parameters:**
- `patterns` (string[], required): File paths or glob patterns (e.g. `["src/auth/**", "package.json"]`)
- `agent` (string, required): Agent identifier (alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores)
- `ttl_minutes` (number, optional): Time-to-live in minutes (default 15)

**Returns:** `{ reserved: true, reservation_ids, agent, patterns, expires_at }` or `{ reserved: false, conflicts }`.

**Example:**
```jsonc
// Input
{ "patterns": ["src/auth/**", "src/middleware/auth.ts"], "agent": "implement-agent-1", "ttl_minutes": 30 }

// Output (success)
{ "reserved": true, "reservation_ids": ["res_..."], "agent": "implement-agent-1", "patterns": ["src/auth/**", "src/middleware/auth.ts"], "expires_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z" }

// Output (conflict)
{ "reserved": false, "conflicts": [{ "pattern": "src/auth/**", "held_by": "implement-agent-2", "expires_at": "2025-01-15T10:15:00.000Z" }] }
```

#### `file_check`
Check for active conflicts on file patterns against other agents' reservations.

**Parameters:**
- `patterns` (string[], required): File paths or glob patterns to check
- `agent` (string, required): Agent checking (excludes own reservations)

**Returns:** `{ has_conflicts, conflicts: [{ pattern, held_by, expires_at }] }`.

**Example:**
```jsonc
// Input
{ "patterns": ["src/auth/jwt.ts"], "agent": "implement-agent-2" }

// Output
{ "has_conflicts": true, "conflicts": [{ "pattern": "src/auth/**", "held_by": "implement-agent-1", "expires_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z" }] }
```

#### `file_release`
Release file reservations for an agent.

**Parameters:**
- `agent` (string, required): Agent identifier (alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores)
- `patterns` (string[], optional): Specific patterns to release. If omitted, releases all reservations for the agent.

**Returns:** `{ released: true, count, agent }`.

## Database Schema

SQLite with WAL mode, foreign keys enabled. Stored at `~/.forgespec/forgespec.db` by default.

### Tables

**contracts** — SDD contract storage
- `id` (TEXT PK), `phase`, `change_name`, `project`, `status`, `confidence` (REAL), `executive_summary`, `data` (JSON TEXT), `created_at`

**boards** — Task board definitions
- `id` (TEXT PK), `project`, `name`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

**tasks** — Individual tasks within boards
- `id` (TEXT PK), `board_id` (FK → boards, CASCADE delete), `title`, `description`, `status`, `priority`, `assignee`, `spec_ref`, `acceptance_criteria`, `dependencies` (JSON array), `notes` (JSON array of {text, timestamp}), `created_at`, `updated_at`, `claimed_at`, `completed_at`

**file_reservations** — Active file locks
- `id` (TEXT PK), `pattern`, `agent`, `expires_at`, `created_at`

**audit_log** — Action audit trail
- `id` (INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT), `timestamp`, `action`, `entity_type`, `entity_id`, `agent_name`, `details`

### Indexes
- `idx_tasks_board` on tasks(board_id)
- `idx_tasks_status` on tasks(status)
- `idx_contracts_project` on contracts(project)
- `idx_reservations_agent` on file_reservations(agent)

### SQLite Configuration
- WAL journal mode for concurrent reads
- Synchronous = NORMAL
- 32MB cache size
- Memory temp store
- 5-second busy timeout

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `FORGESPEC_DIR` | `~/.forgespec` | Database directory |
| `FORGESPEC_DB` | `~/.forgespec/forgespec.db` | Full database path (overrides FORGESPEC_DIR) |

## Project Structure

```
src/
  index.ts              — Entry point: stdio transport bootstrap
  server.ts             — MCP server factory, registers tool groups
  types/index.ts        — Zod schemas, TypeScript types, phase definitions
  database/index.ts     — SQLite initialization, schema creation, WAL config
  tools/
    sdd-contracts.ts    — 6 SDD contract management tools
    task-board.ts       — 10 task board tools with dependency logic
    file-reservation.ts — 3 file reservation tools with pattern matching
  utils/id.ts           — UUID-based ID generation with prefixes
tests/
  sdd-contracts.test.ts — Schema validation, phase transitions, confidence thresholds
  tools.test.ts         — Database tool integration tests
docs/
  assets/
    logo.svg            — ForgeSpec logo
    sdd-pipeline.svg    — SDD pipeline diagram
    architecture.svg    — Architecture overview diagram
```

## Dependencies

### Production
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` ^1.29.0 — MCP protocol implementation
- `better-sqlite3` ^11.8.0 — Embedded SQLite database
- `zod` ^3.23.0 — Runtime schema validation

### Development
- `typescript` ^5.7.0 — TypeScript compiler
- `vitest` ^3.0.0 — Test runner with v8 coverage
- `tsx` ^4.0.0 — TypeScript executor with hot reload
- `standard-version` ^9.5.0 — Automatic semantic versioning from conventional commits
- `@types/better-sqlite3` ^7.6.12 — Type definitions
- `@types/node` ^22.0.0 — Node.js types

## Development

```bash
git clone https://github.com/lleontor705/forgespec-mcp.git
cd forgespec-mcp
npm install
npm run dev       # Run with tsx (hot reload)
npm test          # Run tests with vitest
npm run build     # Compile TypeScript to build/
npm run lint      # Type check (tsc --noEmit)
npm run inspect   # Open MCP Inspector
```

## Versioning

ForgeSpec uses conventional commits with automatic semver bumping via standard-version:

```bash
# Commits determine the version bump:
#   fix: ...    → patch (1.2.0 → 1.2.1)
#   feat: ...   → minor (1.2.0 → 1.3.0)
#   feat!: ...  → major (1.2.0 → 2.0.0)

npm run release            # Auto-bump from commits
npm run release:patch      # Force patch bump
npm run release:minor      # Force minor bump
npm run release:major      # Force major bump

git push --follow-tags origin master   # Triggers CI/CD → npm publish
```
